lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: Tonight, we have a very special guest.
lines.translation: 今晚，我们请到了一位非常特别的嘉宾。

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: Mrs. Anna Sanchez is a three-time Olympic champion and author of the new book To the Edge.
lines.translation: 安娜·桑切斯女士是三届奥运会冠军，也是新书《走向极限》（To the Edge）的作者。

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: Mrs. Sanchez, thank you for joining us.
lines.translation: 桑切斯女士，感谢您参加我们的节目。

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: Thank you for having me.
lines.translation: 谢谢你们邀请我。

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: Let's start with your book.
lines.translation: 让我们从您的书开始谈起吧。

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: What does the title "To the Edge" mean?
lines.translation: 书名“走向极限”是什么意思？

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: What are you referring to?
lines.translation: 您指的是什么？

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: The book is about how science and technology has helped push humans to the edge of their physical abilities.
lines.translation: 这本书是关于科学和技术如何帮助人类突破体能极限的。

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: I argue that in the past 20 years, we have had the best athletes the world has ever seen.
lines.translation: 我认为在过去的20年里，我们拥有了世界上有史以来最出色的运动员。

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: But is this a fair comparison?
lines.translation: 但这是一个公平的比较吗？

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: How do you know how, say, a football player from 50 years ago would compare to one today?
lines.translation: 你怎么知道，比如说，50年前的足球运动员与今天的足球运动员相比如何？

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: Well, you are right.
lines.translation: 嗯，你说得对。

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: That comparison would be perhaps impossible to make.
lines.translation: 这种比较也许是不可能进行的。

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: But the point is more about our knowledge today of human biochemistry, nutrition, and mechanics.
lines.translation: 但重点更多在于我们今天对人体生物化学、营养学和机械学的认识。

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: I believe that while our bodies have not changed in thousands of years, what has changed is the scientific knowledge.
lines.translation: 我相信，虽然我们的身体在几千年来没有变化，但变化的是科学知识。

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: This has allowed athletes to push the limits of what was previously thought possible.
lines.translation: 这使得运动员能够挑战以前认为可能的极限。

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: That's interesting.
lines.translation: 这很有趣。

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: Please tell us more about these perceived limits.
lines.translation: 请告诉我们更多关于这些公认极限的信息。

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: The world is seeing sports records being broken that could only be broken with the aid of technology, whether this be the speed of a tennis serve or the fastest time in a hundred-meter dash or 200-meter swimming race.
lines.translation: 世界正见证着只有在技术辅助下才能打破的体育纪录，无论是网球发球速度，还是百米短跑或200米游泳比赛的最快纪录。

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: Is there any concern that technology is giving some athletes an unfair advantage over others?
lines.translation: 是否有人担心技术会给某些运动员带来相对于他人的不公平优势？

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: That is an interesting question.
lines.translation: 这是一个有趣的问题。

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: And one that has to be considered very carefully.
lines.translation: 这也是一个必须非常谨慎考虑的问题。

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: Skis, for example, went from being made of wood to a metal alloy which allows for better control and faster speed.
lines.translation: 例如，滑雪板从木制的变成了金属合金的，这使得控制力更好，速度更快。

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: There is no stopping technological progress.
lines.translation: 技术进步是无法阻挡的。

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: But, as I said, each situation should be considered carefully on a case by case basis.
lines.translation: 但是，正如我所说，每种情况都应根据具体情况进行仔细考虑。

lines.speaker: Q1.
lines.text: What do we learn about Anna Sanchez?
lines.translation: 关于安娜·桑切斯，我们了解到了什么？

lines.speaker: Q2.
lines.text: What is the woman's book mainly about?
lines.translation: 这位女士的书主要关于什么？

lines.speaker: Q3.
lines.text: What has changed in the past thousands of years?
lines.translation: 在过去的几千年里，什么发生了变化？

lines.speaker: Q4.
lines.text: What is the man's concern about the use of technology in sports competitions?
lines.translation: 男子对在体育比赛中使用技术的担忧是什么？

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: I've worked in international trade all my life.
lines.translation: 我一辈子都在从事国际贸易。

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: My father did so too before me.
lines.translation: 在我之前，我父亲也是这么做的。

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: So I guess you could say it runs in the family.
lines.translation: 所以我想你可以说这是家族传承。

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: What products have you worked with?
lines.translation: 你经营过哪些产品？

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: All sorts, really.
lines.translation: 各种各样的都有。

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: I've imported textiles, machinery, toys, solar panels, all kinds of things over the years.
lines.translation: 这些年来，我进口过纺织品、机械、玩具、太阳能电池板等各种东西。

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: Trends and demand come and go.
lines.translation: 趋势和需求来来去去。

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: So one needs to be very flexible to succeed in this industry.
lines.translation: 所以在这个行业要想成功，必须非常灵活。

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: I see.
lines.translation: 我明白了。

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: What goods are you trading now?
lines.translation: 你现在在交易什么货物？

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: I now import furniture from China into Italy and foods from Italy into China.
lines.translation: 我现在将中国的家具进口到意大利，将意大利的食品进口到中国。

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: I even use the same container.
lines.translation: 我甚至使用同一个集装箱。

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: It's a very efficient way of conducting trade.
lines.translation: 这是一种非常高效的贸易方式。

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: The same container?
lines.translation: 同一个集装箱？

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: You mean you own a 40-foot cargo container?
lines.translation: 你是说你拥有一个40英尺的货运集装箱？

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: Yeah, that's right.
lines.translation: 是的，没错。

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: I have a warehouse in Genoa, Italy and another in Shanghai.
lines.translation: 我在意大利的热那亚有一个仓库，在上海也有一个。

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: I source mid-century modern furniture from different factories in China.
lines.translation: 我从中国不同的工厂采购中世纪现代风格的家具。

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: It's very good value for money.
lines.translation: 物美价廉。

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: I collect it all in my warehouse and then dispatch it to my other warehouse in Italy.
lines.translation: 我把它们全部收集在我的仓库里，然后运送到我在意大利的另一个仓库。

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: Over there I do the same, but with Italian foods instead of furniture, things like pasta, cheese, wine, chocolates.
lines.translation: 在那边我也这样做，只是用意大利食品代替了家具，比如意大利面、奶酪、葡萄酒、巧克力。

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: And I send all that to my warehouse in China in the same freight container I use for the furniture.
lines.translation: 然后我用运输家具的同一个货运集装箱把所有这些都运到我在中国的仓库。

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: So I presume you sell both lines of products wholesale in each respective country.
lines.translation: 所以我推测你在各自的国家批发销售这两个系列的产品。

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: Of course.
lines.translation: 当然。

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: I possess a network of clients and partners in both countries.
lines.translation: 我在两个国家都有客户和合作伙伴网络。

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: That's the main benefit of having done this for so long.
lines.translation: 这就是从事这一行这么久的优势。

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: I've made great business contacts over time.
lines.translation: 随着时间的推移，我建立了很好的商业联系。

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: How many times do you ship?
lines.translation: 你运送过多少次货物？

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: I did 12 shipments last year, 18 this year, and I hope to grow to around 25 next year.
lines.translation: 去年运了12次，今年18次，我希望明年能增加到25次左右。

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: That's both ways, there and back again.
lines.translation: 那是双向的，往返都有。

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: Demand for authentic Italian food in China is growing rapidly.
lines.translation: 中国对地道意大利食品的需求正在迅速增长。

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: And similarly, sales of affordable, yet stylish wooden furniture are also increasing in Italy.
lines.translation: 同样，意大利对价格适中但款式时尚的木制家具的销售也在增加。

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: Furniture is marginally more profitable, mostly because it enjoys lower customs duties.
lines.translation: 家具的利润略高，主要是因为它享有较低的关税。

lines.speaker: Q5.
lines.text: What does the woman think is required to be successful in international trade?
lines.translation: 这位女士认为在国际贸易中取得成功需要什么？

lines.speaker: Q6.
lines.text: What does the woman say is special about her way of doing trade?
lines.translation: 这位女士说她的贸易方式有什么特别之处？

lines.speaker: Q7.
lines.text: What does the woman have in both Italy and China?
lines.translation: 这位女士在意大利和中国都有什么？

lines.speaker: Q8.
lines.text: What does the woman say makes furniture marginally more profitable?
lines.translation: 这位女士说是什么让家具的利润略高？

lines.text: Too many people view their jobs as a five-day prison, which they are paroled every Friday, says Joel Goodman, founder of the Humor Project, a humor consulting group in Saratoga Springs, New York.
lines.translation: 纽约州萨拉托加泉市幽默咨询小组“幽默项目”的创始人乔尔·古德曼说，太多人把工作看成是五天的监狱，每个周五才被假释。

lines.text: Humor unlocks the office prison because it lets adults bring some of their childlike spirit to the job, according to Howard Pollio, professor of psychology at the University of Tennessee Knoxville.
lines.translation: 田纳西大学诺克斯维尔分校心理学教授霍华德·波利奥认为，幽默打开了办公室的监狱大门，因为它让成年人能够带着一些童心去工作。

lines.text: An office with humor breaks is an office with satisfied and productive employees.
lines.translation: 有幽默小休的办公室，其中的员工满意度高且更有效率。

lines.text: Pollio conducted a study that proved humor can help workers excel at routine production tasks.
lines.translation: 波利奥进行的一项研究证明，幽默可以帮助工人在日常生产任务中表现出色。

lines.text: Employees perform better when they have fun.
lines.translation: 员工在快乐工作时表现更好。

lines.text: In large corporations with a hierarchy of power, there is often no outlet for stress.
lines.translation: 在等级严明的大公司里，压力往往没有排解的渠道。

lines.text: Every company needs underground ways of poking fun at the organization, says Lynn Mark, a speaker on workplace humor for Saint Mary's Health Center in St. Louis.
lines.translation: 圣路易斯圣玛丽健康中心的职场幽默演讲者林恩·马克说，每家公司都需要一些非正式的方式来调侃公司机构。

lines.text: Kodak's Rochester, New York branch, discovered a way for its 20,000 employees to uncork their bottled-up resentments.
lines.translation: 柯达公司纽约罗切斯特分公司发现了一种方法，让其2万名员工宣泄积压的怨气。

lines.text: Their 1,000 square foot humor room features a toy store.
lines.translation: 他们1000平方英尺的“幽默室”里有一个玩具店。

lines.text: Among the room's many stress-reducing gadgets, the main attraction is a boss doll with detachable arms and legs.
lines.translation: 在房间里的许多减压小玩意中，最吸引人的是一个四肢可拆卸的老板玩偶。

lines.text: Employees can take the doll apart as long as they put its arms and legs back in place.
lines.translation: 员工可以把玩偶拆开，只要他们最后把四肢装回去就行。

lines.text: Sandy Cohan, owner of a graphic print production business, created the quote board to document the bizarre phrases people say when under strict deadlines.
lines.translation: 平面印刷生产业务负责人桑迪·科恩创建了“语录板”，用来记录人们在紧迫截止日期压力下说出的奇怪话语。

lines.text: "When you're under stress, you say stupid things," says Cohen.
lines.translation: “压力之下，你会说胡话，”科恩说。

lines.text: "Now we just look at each other and say, that's one for the quote board."
lines.translation: “现在我们只是互相看看，然后说：这句该上板了。”

lines.speaker: Q9.
lines.text: What does the passage say about humor in the workplace?
lines.translation: 关于职场幽默，文章说了什么？

lines.speaker: Q10.
lines.text: What does the study by Howard Pollio show?
lines.translation: 霍华德·波利奥的研究表明了什么？

lines.speaker: Q11.
lines.text: What can Kodak's employees do in the humor room?
lines.translation: 柯达的员工在幽默室里可以做什么？

lines.text: Public interest was aroused by the latest discovery of a changed gene in obese mice.
lines.translation: 在肥胖老鼠身上发现变异基因的最新进展引起了公众的关注。

lines.text: The news was made known by Rockefeller University geneticist Jeffrey Friedman.
lines.translation: 这一消息是由洛克菲勒大学遗传学家杰弗里·弗里德曼公布的。

lines.text: The researchers believe this gene influences development of a hormone that tells an organism how fat or full it is.
lines.translation: 研究人员认为，这种基因会影响一种激素的产生，而这种激素会告诉生物体它有多胖或多饱。

lines.text: Those with the changed gene may not sense when they have eaten enough or if they have sufficient fatty tissue, and thus can't tell when to stop eating.
lines.translation: 那些携带变异基因的人可能感觉不到什么时候吃饱了，或者是否有足够的脂肪组织，因此不知道什么时候该停止进食。

lines.text: The researchers also reported finding a gene nearly identical to the mouse obesity gene in humans.
lines.translation: 研究人员还报告说，在人类身上发现了一种与老鼠肥胖基因几乎相同的基因。

lines.text: The operation of this gene in humans has not yet been demonstrated, however.
lines.translation: 然而，这种基因在人体中的运作方式尚未得到证实。

lines.text: Still, professionals like University of Vermont psychologist Eyster Ross Bloom reacted enthusiastically.
lines.translation: 尽管如此，佛蒙特大学心理学家艾斯特·罗斯·布鲁姆等专业人士反应热烈。

lines.text: This research indicates that people really are born with a tendency to have a certain weight, just as they are to have a particular skin color or height.
lines.translation: 这项研究表明，人们天生就有拥有某种体重的倾向，就像他们天生拥有某种肤色或身高一样。

lines.text: Actually, behavioral geneticists believe that less than half of the total weight variation is programmed in the genes, while height is almost entirely genetically determined.
lines.translation: 实际上，行为遗传学家认为，总体重差异中只有不到一半是由基因决定的，而身高几乎完全由遗传决定。

lines.text: Whatever role genes play, Americans are getting fatter.
lines.translation: 无论基因起什么作用，美国人都在变胖。

lines.text: A survey by the Center for Disease Control found that obesity has increased greatly over the last 10 years.
lines.translation: 疾病控制中心的一项调查发现，在过去的10年里，肥胖症大大增加。

lines.text: Such rapid change underlines the role of environmental factors like the abundance of rich foods in Americans' overeating.
lines.translation: 这种快速的变化强调了环境因素的作用，比如丰富的食物导致美国人过度饮食。

lines.text: The Center for Disease Control has also found that teens are far less physically active than they were even a decade ago.
lines.translation: 疾病控制中心还发现，青少年的体力活动远不如十年前。

lines.text: Excepting that weight is predetermined can relieve guilt for overweight people.
lines.translation: 接受体重是预先决定的，可以减轻超重者的罪恶感。

lines.text: But people's belief that they cannot control their weight can itself contribute to obesity.
lines.translation: 但人们认为自己无法控制体重的想法本身也会导致肥胖。

lines.speaker: Q12.
lines.text: What does the speaker say has aroused public interest?
lines.translation: 说话人说是什么引起了公众的关注？

lines.speaker: Q13.
lines.text: What do we learn about the changed gene?
lines.translation: 关于变异基因，我们了解到了什么？

lines.speaker: Q14.
lines.text: What does University of Vermont psychologist Eyster Ross Bloom say?
lines.translation: 佛蒙特大学心理学家艾斯特·罗斯·布鲁姆说了什么？

lines.speaker: Q15.
lines.text: What accounts for Americans' obesity according to a survey by the Center for Disease Control?
lines.translation: 根据疾病控制中心的调查，美国人肥胖的原因是什么？

lines.text: Qualities of a relationship such as openness, compassion and mental stimulation are of concern to most of us regardless of sex.
lines.translation: 无论性别，大多数人都会关注关系中的坦诚、同情心和智力启发等品质。

lines.text: But judging from the questionnaire response, they are more important to women than to men.
lines.translation: 但从问卷调查结果来看，这些品质对女性比对男性更重要。

lines.text: Asked to consider the ingredients of close friendship, women rated these qualities above all others.
lines.translation: 当被要求考虑亲密友谊的成分时，女性将这些品质排在首位。

lines.text: Men assigned a lower priority to them in favor of similarity in interests, selected by 77% of men, and responsiveness in a crisis, chosen by 61% of male respondents.
lines.translation: 男性对这些品质的优先级较低，他们更看重兴趣相似（77%的男性选择）和在危机中的响应（61%的男性选择）。

lines.text: Mental stimulation ranked 3rd in popularity by men as well as women, the only area of overlap.
lines.translation: 智力启发在男性和女性中都排在第3位，这是唯一的重叠领域。

lines.text: Among men, only 28% named openness as an important quality.
lines.translation: 在男性中，只有28%的人认为坦诚是重要的品质。

lines.text: Caring was picked by just 23%.
lines.translation: 只有23%的人选择了体贴。

lines.text: It is evident by their selections that when women speak of close friendships, they are referring to emotional factors.
lines.translation: 从他们的选择中可以明显看出，当女性谈论亲密友谊时，她们指的是情感因素。

lines.text: While men emphasized the pleasure they find in a friend's company.
lines.translation: 而男性则强调从朋友的陪伴中获得的乐趣。

lines.text: That is, when a man speaks of a friend, he is likely to be talking about someone he does things with—a teammate, a fellow hobbyist, a drinking buddy.
lines.translation: 也就是说，当一个男人谈论一个朋友时，他很可能指的是一个和他一起共事的人——一个队友，一个有共同爱好的伙伴，或者一个酒友。

lines.text: These activities are the fabric of the friendship.
lines.translation: 这些活动构成了友谊的基石。

lines.text: It is a "doing" relationship in which similarity in interests is the key bond.
lines.translation: 这是一段“一起做事”的关系，兴趣相似是关键的纽带。

lines.text: This factor was a consideration of less than 11% of women.
lines.translation: 只有不到11%的女性考虑过这一因素。

lines.text: Women opt for a warm, emotional atmosphere where communication flows freely.
lines.translation: 女性选择一种温暖的、充满情感的氛围，其中交流自由顺畅。

lines.text: Activity is mere background.
lines.translation: 活动仅仅是背景。

lines.text: Lastly, men, as we have seen, have serious questions about each other's loyalty.
lines.translation: 最后，正如我们所见，男性对彼此的忠诚度有严重的疑问。

lines.text: Perhaps this is why they placed such strong emphasis on responsiveness in a crisis—someone I can call on for help.
lines.translation: 也许这就是为什么他们如此强调危机中的响应——一个我可以向其求助的人。

lines.text: Women, as their testimonies indicate, are generally more secure with each other and consequently are more likely to treat this issue lightly.
lines.translation: 正如她们的证词所表明的，女性彼此之间通常更有安全感，因此更有可能轻视这个问题。

lines.text: In follow-up interviews, this was confirmed numerous times as woman after woman indicated that being there when needed was taken for granted.
lines.translation: 在随后的访谈中，这一点得到了多次证实，一个又一个女性表示，在需要时出现在身边是理所当然的。

lines.text: As for the hazards of friendship, more than a few relationships have been shattered because of cutthroat competition and feelings of betrayal.
lines.translation: 至于友谊的危机，不少关系因为激烈的竞争和被背叛的感觉而破裂。

lines.text: This applies to both men and women, but unequally.
lines.translation: 这在男性和女性身上都有发生，但程度不同。

lines.text: In comparison, nearly twice as many men complained about these issues as women.
lines.translation: 相比之下，抱怨这些问题的男性几乎是女性的两倍。

lines.text: Further, while competition and betrayal are the main thorns to female friendship, men are plagued in almost equal amounts by two additional issues: lack of frankness and a fear of appearing unmanly.
lines.translation: 此外，虽然竞争和背叛是女性友谊的主要障碍，但男性受到另外两个问题困扰的程度几乎相同：缺乏坦诚和害怕显得不像个男人。

lines.text: Obviously, for a man, a good friendship is hard to find.
lines.translation: 显然，对于一个男人来说，一份好的友谊很难寻觅。

lines.speaker: Q16.
lines.text: What quality do men value most concerning friendship according to a questionnaire response?
lines.translation: 根据问卷调查结果，男性认为友谊中最重要的品质是什么？

lines.speaker: Q17.
lines.text: What do women refer to when speaking of close friendships?
lines.translation: 女性谈论亲密友谊时指的是什么？

lines.speaker: Q18.
lines.text: What may threaten a friendship for both men and women?
lines.translation: 对男性和女性来说，什么可能会威胁到友谊？

lines.text: The partial skeletons of more than 20 dinosaurs and scattered bones of about 300 more have been discovered in Utah and Colorado at what is now the Dinosaur National Monument.
lines.translation: 在现在的恐龙国家纪念地（位于犹他州和科罗拉多州），已经发现了20多具恐龙的部分骨架以及约300具恐龙的零散骨骼。

lines.text: Many of the best specimens may be seen today at museums of natural history in the larger cities of the United States and Canada.
lines.translation: 今天，在美国和加拿大大城市的自然历史博物馆中可以看到许多最好的标本。

lines.text: This dinosaur pit is the largest and best-preserved deposit of dinosaurs known today.
lines.translation: 这个恐龙坑是当今已知最大、保存最完好的恐龙沉积矿床。

lines.text: Many people get the idea from the mass of bones in the pit wall that some disaster, such as a volcanic explosion or a sudden flood, killed a whole herd of dinosaurs in this area.
lines.translation: 许多人从坑壁上大量的骨头中得出这样的看法：某种灾难，如火山爆发或突发洪水，杀死了该地区的整群恐龙。

lines.text: This could have happened, but it probably did not.
lines.translation: 这可能发生过，但可能性不大。

lines.text: The main reasons for thinking otherwise are the scattered bones and the thickness of the deposit.
lines.translation: 之所以有不同想法，主要原因是骨骼散乱且沉积层较厚。

lines.text: In other deposits where the animals were thought to have died together, the skeletons were usually complete and often all the bones were in their proper places.
lines.translation: 在其他被认为动物成批死亡的沉积矿床中，骨架通常是完整的，而且所有的骨头往往都处于正确的位置。

lines.text: Rounded pieces of fossil bone have been found here.
lines.translation: 这里发现了圆形的化石碎片。

lines.text: These fragments got their smooth, round shape by rolling along the stream bottom.
lines.translation: 这些碎片是通过在河底滚动而获得光滑、圆润的外形的。

lines.text: In a mass killing, the bones would have been left on the stream or lake bottom together at the same level.
lines.translation: 在集体死亡中，骨头会被一起留在河底或湖底的同一平面上。

lines.text: But in this deposit, the bones occur throughout a zone of sandstone about 12 feet thick.
lines.translation: 但在该沉积层中，骨骼分布在约12英尺厚的砂岩区。

lines.text: The mixture of swamp dwellers and dry land types also seems to indicate that the deposit is a mixture from different places.
lines.translation: 沼泽居民和旱地类型的混合也似乎表明沉积层是来自不同地方的混合物。

lines.text: The pit area is a large dinosaur graveyard—not a place where they died.
lines.translation: 坑区是一个大型恐龙墓地，而不是它们死亡的地方。

lines.text: Most of the remains probably floated down an eastward flowing river until they were left on a shallow sandbar.
lines.translation: 大部分遗骸可能顺着一条向东流的河漂流，直到留在浅滩上。

lines.text: Some of them may have come from faraway dry land areas to the west.
lines.translation: 其中一些可能来自遥远的西方旱地地区。

lines.text: Perhaps they drowned trying to cross a small stream, or were washed away during floods.
lines.translation: 也许它们是在试图穿过一条小溪时淹死的，或者在洪水期间被冲走的。

lines.text: Some of the swamp dwellers may have got stuck in the very sandbar that became their grave.
lines.translation: 一些沼泽居民可能恰好被困在那个最终成为它们坟墓的沙洲上。

lines.text: Others may have floated for miles before being stranded.
lines.translation: 其他人可能漂流了数英里才搁浅。

lines.text: Even today, similar events take place: When floods come in the spring, sheep, cattle, and deer are often trapped by rising waters and often drown.
lines.translation: 即使在今天，类似的事件也在发生：当春天洪水来临时，羊、牛和鹿经常被上涨的水困住并淹死。

lines.text: Their dead bodies float downstream until the flood recedes, and leaves them stranded on a bar or shore where they lie, half buried in the sand until they decay.
lines.translation: 它们的尸体顺流而下，直到洪水退去，让它们搁浅在沙洲或岸边，半埋在沙中直到腐烂。

lines.text: Early travelers on the Missouri River reported that shores and bars were often lined with the decaying bodies of buffalo that had died during spring floods.
lines.translation: 密苏里河早期的旅行者报告说，岸边和沙洲上经常排列着死于春季洪水的野牛腐烂的尸体。

lines.speaker: Q19.
lines.text: Where can many of the best dinosaur specimens be found in North America?
lines.translation: 在北美的哪里可以看到许多最好的恐龙标本？

lines.speaker: Q20.
lines.text: What occurs to many people when they see the massive bones in the pit wall?
lines.translation: 当许多人看到坑壁上大量的骨头时，他们会想到什么？

lines.speaker: Q21.
lines.text: What does the speaker suggest about the large number of dinosaur bones found in the pit?
lines.translation: 关于坑中发现的大量恐龙骨骼，说话人暗示了什么？

lines.text: I would like particularly to talk about the need to develop a new style of aging in our own society.
lines.translation: 我特别想谈谈在我们自己的社会中发展一种新的老龄化模式的必要性。

lines.text: Young people in this country have been accused of not caring for their parents the way they would have in the old country.
lines.translation: 这个国家的年轻人被指责没有像在故乡那样照顾他们的父母。

lines.text: And this is true.
lines.translation: 事实确实如此。

lines.text: But it is also true that old people have been influenced by an American ideal of independence and autonomy.
lines.translation: 但同样事实是，老年人也受到美国独立的、自主的理想的影响。

lines.text: So we live alone, perhaps on the verge of starvation, in time without friends.
lines.translation: 所以我们独自生活，也许濒临饥饿，久而久之没有朋友。

lines.text: But we are independent.
lines.translation: 但我们是独立的。

lines.text: This standard American style has been forced on every ethnic group.
lines.translation: 这种标准的美国风格被强加给了每一个族群。

lines.text: Although there are many groups for whom the ideal is not practical, it is a poor ideal and pursuing it does a great deal of harm.
lines.translation: 尽管对许多群体来说这种理想并不切实际，但这是一个糟糕的理想，追求它会带来巨大的伤害。

lines.text: This ideal of independence also contains a tremendous amount of unselfishness.
lines.translation: 这种独立的理想也包含着极大的无私。

lines.text: In talking to today's young mothers, I have asked them what kind of grandmothers they think they are going to be.
lines.translation: 在与当今的年轻母亲交谈时，我问过她们，她们认为自己会成为什么样的祖母。

lines.text: I hear devoted loving mothers say that when they are through raising their children, they have no intention of becoming grandmothers.
lines.translation: 我听到尽职尽责、充满爱心的母亲说，当她们抚养完孩子后，她们无意成为祖母。

lines.text: They are astonished to hear that in most of the world, throughout most of its history, families have been three or four generation-families living under the same roof.
lines.translation: 她们惊讶地听说，在世界上大多数地方，在历史的大部分时间里，家庭都是三四代人同住一个屋檐下。

lines.text: We have over-emphasized the small family unit—father, mother, small children.
lines.translation: 我们过度强调了小家庭单位——父亲、母亲、小孩。

lines.text: We think it is wonderful if grandma and grandpa, if they're still alive, can live alone.
lines.translation: 如果爷爷奶奶（如果他们还活着的话）能单独生活，我们认为那是极好的。

lines.text: We have reached the point where we think the only thing we can do for our children is to stay out of their way.
lines.translation: 我们已经到了认为我们能为孩子做的唯一事情就是不去妨碍他们的地步。

lines.text: The only thing we can do for our daughter-in-law is to see as little of her as possible.
lines.translation: 我们能为儿媳做的唯一事情就是尽可能少见到她。

lines.text: Old people's nursing homes, even the best ones, are filled with older people who believe the only thing they can do for their children is to look cheerful when they come to visit.
lines.translation: 老人院，即使是最好的老人院，也住满了相信他们能为孩子做的唯一事情就是在孩子来访时显得开心的老人。

lines.text: So in the end, older people have to devote all their energies to not being a burden.
lines.translation: 所以到头来，老年人不得不全身心地投入到不成为负担中。

lines.text: We are beginning to see what a tremendous price we've paid for our emphasis on independence and autonomy.
lines.translation: 我们开始看到，我们为强调独立和自主付出了多么巨大的代价。

lines.text: We've isolated old people and we've cut off the children from their grandparents.
lines.translation: 我们孤立了老人，切断了孩子与祖父母的联系。

lines.text: One of the reasons we have as bad a generation gap today as we do is that grandparents have stepped out.
lines.translation: 今天代沟如此严重的原因之一就是祖父母退出了家庭生活。

lines.text: Young people are being deprived of the thing they need most: perspective—to know why their parents behave so peculiarly and why their grandparents say the things they do.
lines.translation: 年轻人正在被剥夺他们最需要的东西：视角——去了解为什么他们的父母行为如此古怪，以及为什么他们的祖父母会说那些话。

lines.speaker: Q22.
lines.text: What have young Americans been accused of?
lines.translation: 美国年轻人被指责什么？

lines.speaker: Q23.
lines.text: What does the speaker say about old people in the United States?
lines.translation: 说话人关于美国的退休老人说了什么？

lines.speaker: Q24.
lines.text: What is astonishing to the young mothers interviewed by the speaker?
lines.translation: 对说话人采访的年轻母亲来说，什么是令人惊讶的？

lines.speaker: Q25.
lines.text: What does the speaker say older people try their best to do?
lines.translation: 说话人说老年人尽最大努力做什么？
